{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01LzMok8PZKAyn1wWkQFvfLu","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_012nL23QpLNTwkc6pxHWhNaR","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/regression_test.go","content":"/*\nCopyright 2025 Gravitational, Inc.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n*/\n\npackage main\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"encoding/base64\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\n\t\"github.com/jonboulle/clockwork\"\n\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\"\n\t\"github.com/tstranex/u2f\"\n\n\t\"github.com/gravitational/teleport\"\n\t\"github.com/gravitational/teleport/api/client/proto\"\n\t\"github.com/gravitational/teleport/api/types\"\n\t\"github.com/gravitational/teleport/lib/auth\"\n\t\"github.com/gravitational/teleport/lib/auth/mocku2f\"\n\tu2flib \"github.com/gravitational/teleport/lib/auth/u2f\"\n\t\"github.com/gravitational/teleport/lib/services\"\n)\n\n// TestRegression_MultiDeviceU2FAuthentication verifies that multi-device U2F\n// authentication is properly supported. This test checks:\n//\n// 1. That U2F challenges include ALL registered U2F devices (not just the first one)\n// 2. That users can authenticate with any of their registered devices\n// 3. That the newer MFA API correctly handles multiple U2F devices\n// 4. That device management operations work correctly with multiple devices\nfunc TestRegression_MultiDeviceU2FAuthentication(t *testing.T) {\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\tclock := clockwork.NewFakeClock()\n\n\t// Set up test auth server with U2F enabled\n\tas, err := auth.NewTestAuthServer(auth.TestAuthServerConfig{\n\t\tDir:   t.TempDir(),\n\t\tClock: clock,\n\t})\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\tdefer as.Close()\n\n\t// Enable U2F support\n\tauthPref, err := services.NewAuthPreference(types.AuthPreferenceSpecV2{\n\t\tType: teleport.Local,\n\t\tU2F: &types.U2F{\n\t\t\tAppID:  \"teleport\",\n\t\t\tFacets: []string{\"teleport\"},\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\terr = as.AuthServer.SetAuthPreference(authPref)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t// Create a test user\n\tuser, _, err := auth.CreateUserAndRole(as.AuthServer, \"mfa-user\", []string{\"role\"})\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t// Create a gRPC client for the user\n\tsrv, err := as.NewTestTLSServer()\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\tdefer srv.Close()\n\n\tcl, err := srv.NewClient(auth.TestUser(user.GetName()))\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\tdefer cl.Close()\n\n\t// Register multiple U2F devices\n\tu2fDevices := make(map[string]*mocku2f.Key)\n\tu2fRegistrations := make(map[string]*types.MFADevice)\n\n\tdeviceNames := []string{\"u2f-device-1\", \"u2f-device-2\", \"u2f-device-3\"}\n\n\tfor _, deviceName := range deviceNames {\n\t\t// Step 1: Initiate device registration\n\t\tstream, err := cl.AddMFADevice(ctx)\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\terr = stream.Send(&proto.AddMFADeviceRequest{\n\t\t\tRequest: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequest_Init{\n\t\t\t\tInit: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequestInit{\n\t\t\t\t\tDeviceName: deviceName,\n\t\t\t\t\tType:       proto.AddMFADeviceRequestInit_U2F,\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Step 2: Receive registration challenge\n\t\tresp, err := stream.Recv()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\tregChallenge := resp.GetMFAChallenge().GetU2F()\n\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, regChallenge, \"Expected U2F registration challenge for device %s\", deviceName)\n\n\t\t// Step 3: Create mock U2F device and respond to challenge\n\t\tmockDev, err := mocku2f.Create()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\tu2fDevices[deviceName] = mockDev\n\n\t\tregResp, err := mockDev.RegisterResponse(&u2f.RegisterChallenge{\n\t\t\tChallenge: regChallenge.Challenge,\n\t\t\tAppID:     regChallenge.AppID,\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Step 4: Send registration response\n\t\terr = stream.Send(&proto.AddMFADeviceRequest{\n\t\t\tRequest: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequest_RegisterResponse{\n\t\t\t\tRegisterResponse: &proto.MFARegisterResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\tResponse: &proto.MFARegisterResponse_U2F{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tU2F: &proto.U2FRegisterResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tRegistrationData: regResp.RegistrationData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tClientData:       regResp.ClientData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Step 5: Receive device details\n\t\tresp, err = stream.Recv()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\tdevice := resp.GetDevice()\n\t\trequire.NotNil(t, device, \"Expected device registration response for %s\", deviceName)\n\t\tu2fRegistrations[deviceName] = device\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify all devices were registered\n\tdevices, err := cl.GetMFADevices(ctx, &proto.GetMFADevicesRequest{})\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\trequire.Len(t, devices.Devices, 3, \"Expected 3 U2F devices to be registered\")\n\n\t// TEST 1: Verify OLD API (U2FSignRequest) - should expose the bug\n\tt.Run(\"OldAPI_SingleDeviceChallenge_BUG\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// The old U2FSignRequest API returns only ONE challenge (for the first device found)\n\t\t// This is the bug - it should return challenges for ALL devices\n\t\t//\n\t\t// We can't directly call U2FSignRequest from here, but we can verify through the\n\t\t// auth server that it has this limitation.\n\t\t// The bug is in: lib/auth/auth.go U2FSignRequest() func at line 828-864\n\t\t//\n\t\t// The function has this code:\n\t\t//   for _, dev := range devs {\n\t\t//       if dev.GetU2F() == nil { continue }\n\t\t//       return u2f.AuthenticateInit(...) // BUG: returns immediately on first device\n\t\t//   }\n\t\t//\n\t\t// This means only the first device gets a challenge, users can't choose which device to use.\n\n\t\t// Verify that U2FSignRequest only handles the first device\n\t\t// by checking the behavior through the auth server\n\t\tdevList, err := as.AuthServer.GetMFADevices(ctx, user.GetName())\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\trequire.Len(t, devList, 3)\n\n\t\t// The old API would only return a challenge for the first U2F device\n\t\t// To properly test this, we'd need to call U2FSignRequest, but it requires\n\t\t// a valid password which we don't have in this test context.\n\t\t// However, the bug is confirmed by examining the code:\n\t\t// - U2FSignRequest has a TODO comment at line 847 saying \"support challenge with multiple devices\"\n\t\t// - The loop returns immediately on the first device instead of collecting all devices\n\t})\n\n\t// TEST 2: Verify NEW API (mfaAuthChallenge) - should work correctly\n\tt.Run(\"NewAPI_MultiDeviceChallenge_CORRECT\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// The new mfaAuthChallenge API correctly supports multiple devices\n\t\t// Create an in-memory U2F storage for authentication\n\t\tu2fStorage, err := u2flib.InMemoryAuthenticationStorage(as.AuthServer.Identity)\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Get MFA challenges for all devices\n\t\tchallenges, err := as.AuthServer.(*auth.Server).GetMFAAuthChallenge(ctx, user.GetName(), u2fStorage)\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Verify that we got challenges for ALL 3 U2F devices\n\t\trequire.NotNil(t, challenges, \"Expected MFA challenges\")\n\t\trequire.Len(t, challenges.U2F, 3, \"Expected 3 U2F challenges (one for each device)\")\n\n\t\t// Verify each challenge is unique and corresponds to a device\n\t\tseenKeyHandles := make(map[string]bool)\n\t\tfor i, challenge := range challenges.U2F {\n\t\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, challenge.KeyHandle, \"Challenge %d has empty key handle\", i)\n\t\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, challenge.Challenge, \"Challenge %d has empty challenge\", i)\n\t\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, challenge.AppID, \"Challenge %d has empty AppID\", i)\n\n\t\t\t// Check for uniqueness of key handles\n\t\t\trequire.False(t, seenKeyHandles[challenge.KeyHandle],\n\t\t\t\t\"Duplicate key handle in challenge %d\", i)\n\t\t\tseenKeyHandles[challenge.KeyHandle] = true\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Verify we can authenticate with any of the devices\n\t\tfor i, deviceName := range deviceNames {\n\t\t\tchallenge := challenges.U2F[i]\n\n\t\t\t// Get the key handle as bytes to match against the device\n\t\t\tkeyHandleBytes, err := base64.URLEncoding.WithPadding(base64.NoPadding).DecodeString(challenge.KeyHandle)\n\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t\tmockDev := u2fDevices[deviceName]\n\t\t\trequire.Equal(t, mockDev.KeyHandle, keyHandleBytes,\n\t\t\t\t\"Key handle mismatch for device %s at challenge %d\", deviceName, i)\n\n\t\t\t// Create a sign response using the mock device\n\t\t\tsignResp, err := mockDev.SignResponse(&u2f.AuthenticateChallenge{\n\t\t\t\tChallenge: challenge.Challenge,\n\t\t\t\tAppID:     challenge.AppID,\n\t\t\t\tKeyHandle: challenge.KeyHandle,\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t\t// Verify the response can be validated\n\t\t\tauthResp := u2f.AuthenticateChallengeResponse{\n\t\t\t\tKeyHandle:     signResp.KeyHandle,\n\t\t\t\tSignatureData: signResp.SignatureData,\n\t\t\t\tClientData:    signResp.ClientData,\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// This should succeed for any of the registered devices\n\t\t\terr = as.AuthServer.(*auth.Server).CheckU2FSignResponse(ctx, user.GetName(), &authResp)\n\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err, \"Failed to verify sign response for device %s\", deviceName)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\t// TEST 3: Verify device management with multiple devices\n\tt.Run(\"DeviceManagement_MultipleDevices\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Test adding a new device when multiple already exist\n\t\tstream, err := cl.AddMFADevice(ctx)\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\tdefer stream.CloseSend()\n\n\t\t// Initiate registration for a 4th device\n\t\terr = stream.Send(&proto.AddMFADeviceRequest{\n\t\t\tRequest: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequest_Init{\n\t\t\t\tInit: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequestInit{\n\t\t\t\t\tDeviceName: \"u2f-device-4\",\n\t\t\t\t\tType:       proto.AddMFADeviceRequestInit_U2F,\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Should receive an existing MFA challenge (to authenticate before adding new device)\n\t\tresp, err := stream.Recv()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\texistingChallenge := resp.GetExistingMFAChallenge()\n\t\trequire.NotNil(t, existingChallenge, \"Expected existing MFA challenge before adding new device\")\n\n\t\t// With multiple devices, the challenge should include all 3 existing devices\n\t\trequire.Len(t, existingChallenge.U2F, 3,\n\t\t\t\"Expected challenges for all 3 existing U2F devices when adding a new device\")\n\n\t\t// Respond with authentication using the first device\n\t\tdevice1Challenge := existingChallenge.U2F[0]\n\t\tdevice1 := u2fDevices[deviceNames[0]]\n\n\t\tsignResp, err := device1.SignResponse(&u2f.AuthenticateChallenge{\n\t\t\tChallenge: device1Challenge.Challenge,\n\t\t\tAppID:     device1Challenge.AppID,\n\t\t\tKeyHandle: device1Challenge.KeyHandle,\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\terr = stream.Send(&proto.AddMFADeviceRequest{\n\t\t\tRequest: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequest_ExistingMFAResponse{\n\t\t\t\tExistingMFAResponse: &proto.MFAAuthenticateResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\tResponse: &proto.MFAAuthenticateResponse_U2F{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tU2F: &proto.U2FResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tKeyHandle:  signResp.KeyHandle,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tClientData: signResp.ClientData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tSignature:  signResp.SignatureData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Receive registration challenge for the new device\n\t\tresp, err = stream.Recv()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\tregChallenge := resp.GetMFAChallenge().GetU2F()\n\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, regChallenge)\n\n\t\t// Register the new device\n\t\tdevice4, err := mocku2f.Create()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\tregResp, err := device4.RegisterResponse(&u2f.RegisterChallenge{\n\t\t\tChallenge: regChallenge.Challenge,\n\t\t\tAppID:     regChallenge.AppID,\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\terr = stream.Send(&proto.AddMFADeviceRequest{\n\t\t\tRequest: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequest_RegisterResponse{\n\t\t\t\tRegisterResponse: &proto.MFARegisterResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\tResponse: &proto.MFARegisterResponse_U2F{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tU2F: &proto.U2FRegisterResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tRegistrationData: regResp.RegistrationData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tClientData:       regResp.ClientData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Receive final confirmation\n\t\tresp, err = stream.Recv()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\trequire.NotNil(t, resp.GetDevice())\n\n\t\t// Verify all 4 devices are now registered\n\t\tdevices, err := cl.GetMFADevices(ctx, &proto.GetMFADevicesRequest{})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\trequire.Len(t, devices.Devices, 4, \"Expected 4 U2F devices after adding 4th device\")\n\t})\n\n\t// TEST 4: Verify challenge for deleting a device when multiple exist\n\tt.Run(\"DeleteDevice_WithMultipleDevices\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Create a stream to delete a device\n\t\tstream, err := cl.DeleteMFADevice(ctx)\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\tdefer stream.CloseSend()\n\n\t\t// Initiate deletion of device 2\n\t\terr = stream.Send(&proto.DeleteMFADeviceRequest{\n\t\t\tRequest: &proto.DeleteMFADeviceRequest_Init{\n\t\t\t\tInit: &proto.DeleteMFADeviceRequestInit{\n\t\t\t\t\tDeviceName: \"u2f-device-2\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Receive MFA challenge for authentication\n\t\tresp, err := stream.Recv()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\tdeleteChallenge := resp.GetMFAChallenge()\n\t\trequire.NotNil(t, deleteChallenge)\n\n\t\t// The challenge should include challenges for ALL remaining devices\n\t\t// (at this point we should have 4 devices, so 4 U2F challenges)\n\t\trequire.Len(t, deleteChallenge.U2F, 4,\n\t\t\t\"Expected challenges for all 4 U2F devices when deleting a device\")\n\n\t\t// Respond with authentication using the first device\n\t\tdevice1Challenge := deleteChallenge.U2F[0]\n\t\tdevice1 := u2fDevices[deviceNames[0]]\n\n\t\tsignResp, err := device1.SignResponse(&u2f.AuthenticateChallenge{\n\t\t\tChallenge: device1Challenge.Challenge,\n\t\t\tAppID:     device1Challenge.AppID,\n\t\t\tKeyHandle: device1Challenge.KeyHandle,\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\terr = stream.Send(&proto.DeleteMFADeviceRequest{\n\t\t\tRequest: signResp.SignatureData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Receive acknowledgment\n\t\tresp, err = stream.Recv()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, \"u2f-device-2\", dev.Metadata.Name,\n\t\t\t\t\"Device 'u2f-device-2' should have been deleted\")\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\t// TEST 5: Verify client/server version compatibility\n\tt.Run(\"ClientServerCompatibility_U2FChallenges\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Test that the challenge format is compatible between old and new clients\n\t\tu2fStorage, err := This test checks:\n//\n// 1. That U2F challenges include ALL registered U2F devices (not just the first one)\n// 2. That users can authenticate with any of their registered devices\n// 3. That the newer MFA API correctly handles multiple U2F devices\n// 4. That device management operations work correctly with multiple devices\nfunc TestRegression_MultiDeviceU2FAuthentication(t *testing.T) {\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\tclock := clockwork.NewFakeClock()\n\n\t// Set up test auth server with U2F enabled\n\tas, err := auth.NewTestAuthServer(auth.TestAuthServerConfig{\n\t\tDir:   t.TempDir(),\n\t\tClock: clock,\n\t})\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\tdefer as.Close()\n\n\t// Enable U2F support\n\tauthPref, err := services.NewAuthPreference(types.AuthPreferenceSpecV2{\n\t\tType: teleport.Local,\n\t\tU2F: &types.U2F{\n\t\t\tAppID:  \"teleport\",\n\t\t\tFacets: []string{\"teleport\"},\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\terr = as.AuthServer.SetAuthPreference(authPref)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t// Create a test user\n\tuser, _, err := auth.CreateUserAndRole(as.AuthServer, \"mfa-user\", []string{\"role\"})\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t// Create a gRPC client for the user\n\tsrv, err := as.NewTestTLSServer()\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\tdefer srv.Close()\n\n\tcl, err := srv.NewClient(auth.TestUser(user.GetName()))\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\tdefer cl.Close()\n\n\t// Register multiple U2F devices\n\tu2fDevices := make(map[string]*mocku2f.Key)\n\tu2fRegistrations := make(map[string]*types.MFADevice)\n\n\tdeviceNames := []string{\"u2f-device-1\", \"u2f-device-2\", \"u2f-device-3\"}\n\n\tfor _, deviceName := range deviceNames {\n\t\t// Step 1: Initiate device registration\n\t\tstream, err := cl.AddMFADevice(ctx)\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\terr = stream.Send(&proto.AddMFADeviceRequest{\n\t\t\tRequest: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequest_Init{\n\t\t\t\tInit: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequestInit{\n\t\t\t\t\tDeviceName: deviceName,\n\t\t\t\t\tType:       proto.AddMFADeviceRequestInit_U2F,\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Step 2: Receive registration challenge\n\t\tresp, err := stream.Recv()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\tregChallenge := resp.GetMFAChallenge().GetU2F()\n\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, regChallenge, \"Expected U2F registration challenge for device %s\", deviceName)\n\n\t\t// Step 3: Create mock U2F device and respond to challenge\n\t\tmockDev, err := mocku2f.Create()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\tu2fDevices[deviceName] = mockDev\n\n\t\tregResp, err := mockDev.RegisterResponse(&u2f.RegisterChallenge{\n\t\t\tChallenge: regChallenge.Challenge,\n\t\t\tAppID:     regChallenge.AppID,\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Step 4: Send registration response\n\t\terr = stream.Send(&proto.AddMFADeviceRequest{\n\t\t\tRequest: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequest_RegisterResponse{\n\t\t\t\tRegisterResponse: &proto.MFARegisterResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\tResponse: &proto.MFARegisterResponse_U2F{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tU2F: &proto.U2FRegisterResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tRegistrationData: regResp.RegistrationData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tClientData:       regResp.ClientData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Step 5: Receive device details\n\t\tresp, err = stream.Recv()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\tdevice := resp.GetDevice()\n\t\trequire.NotNil(t, device, \"Expected device registration response for %s\", deviceName)\n\t\tu2fRegistrations[deviceName] = device\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify all devices were registered\n\tdevices, err := cl.GetMFADevices(ctx, &proto.GetMFADevicesRequest{})\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\trequire.Len(t, devices.Devices, 3, \"Expected 3 U2F devices to be registered\")\n\n\t// TEST 1: Verify OLD API (U2FSignRequest) - should expose the bug\n\tt.Run(\"OldAPI_SingleDeviceChallenge_BUG\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// The old U2FSignRequest API returns only ONE challenge (for the first device found)\n\t\t// This is the bug - it should return challenges for ALL devices\n\t\t//\n\t\t// We can't directly call U2FSignRequest from here, but we can verify through the\n\t\t// auth server that it has this limitation.\n\t\t// The bug is in: lib/auth/auth.go U2FSignRequest() func at line 828-864\n\t\t//\n\t\t// The function has this code:\n\t\t//   for _, dev := range devs {\n\t\t//       if dev.GetU2F() == nil { continue }\n\t\t//       return u2f.AuthenticateInit(...) // BUG: returns immediately on first device\n\t\t//   }\n\t\t//\n\t\t// This means only the first device gets a challenge, users can't choose which device to use.\n\n\t\t// Verify that U2FSignRequest only handles the first device\n\t\t// by checking the behavior through the auth server\n\t\tdevList, err := as.AuthServer.GetMFADevices(ctx, user.GetName())\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\trequire.Len(t, devList, 3)\n\n\t\t// The old API would only return a challenge for the first U2F device\n\t\t// To properly test this, we'd need to call U2FSignRequest, but it requires\n\t\t// a valid password which we don't have in this test context.\n\t\t// However, the bug is confirmed by examining the code:\n\t\t// - U2FSignRequest has a TODO comment at line 847 saying \"support challenge with multiple devices\"\n\t\t// - The loop returns immediately on the first device instead of collecting all devices\n\t})\n\n\t// TEST 2: Verify NEW API (mfaAuthChallenge) - should work correctly\n\tt.Run(\"NewAPI_MultiDeviceChallenge_CORRECT\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// The new mfaAuthChallenge API correctly supports multiple devices\n\t\t// Create an in-memory U2F storage for authentication\n\t\tu2fStorage, err := u2flib.InMemoryAuthenticationStorage(as.AuthServer.Identity)\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Get MFA challenges for all devices\n\t\tchallenges, err := as.AuthServer.(*auth.Server).GetMFAAuthChallenge(ctx, user.GetName(), u2fStorage)\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Verify that we got challenges for ALL 3 U2F devices\n\t\trequire.NotNil(t, challenges, \"Expected MFA challenges\")\n\t\trequire.Len(t, challenges.U2F, 3, \"Expected 3 U2F challenges (one for each device)\")\n\n\t\t// Verify each challenge is unique and corresponds to a device\n\t\tseenKeyHandles := make(map[string]bool)\n\t\tfor i, challenge := range challenges.U2F {\n\t\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, challenge.KeyHandle, \"Challenge %d has empty key handle\", i)\n\t\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, challenge.Challenge, \"Challenge %d has empty challenge\", i)\n\t\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, challenge.AppID, \"Challenge %d has empty AppID\", i)\n\n\t\t\t// Check for uniqueness of key handles\n\t\t\trequire.False(t, seenKeyHandles[challenge.KeyHandle],\n\t\t\t\t\"Duplicate key handle in challenge %d\", i)\n\t\t\tseenKeyHandles[challenge.KeyHandle] = true\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Verify we can authenticate with any of the devices\n\t\tfor i, deviceName := range deviceNames {\n\t\t\tchallenge := challenges.U2F[i]\n\n\t\t\t// Get the key handle as bytes to match against the device\n\t\t\tkeyHandleBytes, err := base64.URLEncoding.WithPadding(base64.NoPadding).DecodeString(challenge.KeyHandle)\n\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t\tmockDev := u2fDevices[deviceName]\n\t\t\trequire.Equal(t, mockDev.KeyHandle, keyHandleBytes,\n\t\t\t\t\"Key handle mismatch for device %s at challenge %d\", deviceName, i)\n\n\t\t\t// Create a sign response using the mock device\n\t\t\tsignResp, err := mockDev.SignResponse(&u2f.AuthenticateChallenge{\n\t\t\t\tChallenge: challenge.Challenge,\n\t\t\t\tAppID:     challenge.AppID,\n\t\t\t\tKeyHandle: challenge.KeyHandle,\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t\t// Verify the response can be validated\n\t\t\tauthResp := u2f.AuthenticateChallengeResponse{\n\t\t\t\tKeyHandle:     signResp.KeyHandle,\n\t\t\t\tSignatureData: signResp.SignatureData,\n\t\t\t\tClientData:    signResp.ClientData,\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// This should succeed for any of the registered devices\n\t\t\terr = as.AuthServer.(*auth.Server).CheckU2FSignResponse(ctx, user.GetName(), &authResp)\n\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err, \"Failed to verify sign response for device %s\", deviceName)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\t// TEST 3: Verify device management with multiple devices\n\tt.Run(\"DeviceManagement_MultipleDevices\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Test adding a new device when multiple already exist\n\t\tstream, err := cl.AddMFADevice(ctx)\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\tdefer stream.CloseSend()\n\n\t\t// Initiate registration for a 4th device\n\t\terr = stream.Send(&proto.AddMFADeviceRequest{\n\t\t\tRequest: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequest_Init{\n\t\t\t\tInit: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequestInit{\n\t\t\t\t\tDeviceName: \"u2f-device-4\",\n\t\t\t\t\tType:       proto.AddMFADeviceRequestInit_U2F,\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Should receive an existing MFA challenge (to authenticate before adding new device)\n\t\tresp, err := stream.Recv()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\texistingChallenge := resp.GetExistingMFAChallenge()\n\t\trequire.NotNil(t, existingChallenge, \"Expected existing MFA challenge before adding new device\")\n\n\t\t// With multiple devices, the challenge should include all 3 existing devices\n\t\trequire.Len(t, existingChallenge.U2F, 3,\n\t\t\t\"Expected challenges for all 3 existing U2F devices when adding a new device\")\n\n\t\t// Respond with authentication using the first device\n\t\tdevice1Challenge := existingChallenge.U2F[0]\n\t\tdevice1 := u2fDevices[deviceNames[0]]\n\n\t\tsignResp, err := device1.SignResponse(&u2f.AuthenticateChallenge{\n\t\t\tChallenge: device1Challenge.Challenge,\n\t\t\tAppID:     device1Challenge.AppID,\n\t\t\tKeyHandle: device1Challenge.KeyHandle,\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\terr = stream.Send(&proto.AddMFADeviceRequest{\n\t\t\tRequest: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequest_ExistingMFAResponse{\n\t\t\t\tExistingMFAResponse: &proto.MFAAuthenticateResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\tResponse: &proto.MFAAuthenticateResponse_U2F{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tU2F: &proto.U2FResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tKeyHandle:  signResp.KeyHandle,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tClientData: signResp.ClientData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tSignature:  signResp.SignatureData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Receive registration challenge for the new device\n\t\tresp, err = regChallenge.AppID,\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\terr = stream.Send(&proto.AddMFADeviceRequest{\n\t\t\tRequest: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequest_RegisterResponse{\n\t\t\t\tRegisterResponse: &proto.MFARegisterResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\tResponse: &proto.MFARegisterResponse_U2F{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tU2F: &proto.U2FRegisterResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tRegistrationData: regResp.RegistrationData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tClientData:       regResp.ClientData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Receive final u2flib.InMemoryAuthenticationStorage(as.AuthServer.Identity)\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\tchallenges, err := as.AuthServer.(*auth.Server).GetMFAAuthChallenge(ctx, user.GetName(), u2fStorage)\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// The proto structure should support multiple U2F challenges\n\t\trequire.Len(t, challenges.U2F, 3)\n\n\t\t// Each challenge should be properly That the newer MFA API correctly handles multiple U2F devices\n// 4. That device management operations work correctly with multiple devices\nfunc TestRegression_MultiDeviceU2FAuthentication(t *testing.T) {\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\tclock := clockwork.NewFakeClock()\n\n\t// Set up test auth server with U2F enabled\n\tas, err := auth.NewTestAuthServer(auth.TestAuthServerConfig{\n\t\tDir:   t.TempDir(),\n\t\tClock: clock,\n\t})\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\tdefer as.Close()\n\n\t// Enable U2F support\n\tauthPref, err := services.NewAuthPreference(types.AuthPreferenceSpecV2{\n\t\tType: teleport.Local,\n\t\tU2F: &types.U2F{\n\t\t\tAppID:  \"teleport\",\n\t\t\tFacets: []string{\"teleport\"},\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\terr = as.AuthServer.SetAuthPreference(authPref)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t// Create a test user\n\tuser, _, err := auth.CreateUserAndRole(as.AuthServer, \"mfa-user\", []string{\"role\"})\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t// Create a gRPC client for the user\n\tsrv, err := as.NewTestTLSServer()\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\tdefer srv.Close()\n\n\tcl, err := srv.NewClient(auth.TestUser(user.GetName()))\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\tdefer cl.Close()\n\n\t// Register multiple U2F devices\n\tu2fDevices := make(map[string]*mocku2f.Key)\n\tu2fRegistrations := make(map[string]*types.MFADevice)\n\n\tdeviceNames := []string{\"u2f-device-1\", \"u2f-device-2\", \"u2f-device-3\"}\n\n\tfor _, deviceName := range deviceNames {\n\t\t// Step 1: Initiate device registration\n\t\tstream, err := cl.AddMFADevice(ctx)\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\terr = stream.Send(&proto.AddMFADeviceRequest{\n\t\t\tRequest: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequest_Init{\n\t\t\t\tInit: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequestInit{\n\t\t\t\t\tDeviceName: deviceName,\n\t\t\t\t\tType:       proto.AddMFADeviceRequestInit_U2F,\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Step 2: Receive registration challenge\n\t\tresp, err := stream.Recv()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\tregChallenge := resp.GetMFAChallenge().GetU2F()\n\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, regChallenge, \"Expected U2F registration challenge for device %s\", deviceName)\n\n\t\t// Step 3: Create mock U2F device and respond to challenge\n\t\tmockDev, err := mocku2f.Create()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\tu2fDevices[deviceName] = mockDev\n\n\t\tregResp, err := mockDev.RegisterResponse(&u2f.RegisterChallenge{\n\t\t\tChallenge: regChallenge.Challenge,\n\t\t\tAppID:     regChallenge.AppID,\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Step 4: Send registration response\n\t\terr = stream.Send(&proto.AddMFADeviceRequest{\n\t\t\tRequest: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequest_RegisterResponse{\n\t\t\t\tRegisterResponse: &proto.MFARegisterResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\tResponse: &proto.MFARegisterResponse_U2F{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tU2F: &proto.U2FRegisterResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tRegistrationData: regResp.RegistrationData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tClientData:       regResp.ClientData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Step 5: Receive device details\n\t\tresp, err = stream.Recv()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\tdevice := resp.GetDevice()\n\t\trequire.NotNil(t, device, \"Expected device registration response for %s\", deviceName)\n\t\tu2fRegistrations[deviceName] = device\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify all devices were registered\n\tdevices, err := cl.GetMFADevices(ctx, &proto.GetMFADevicesRequest{})\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\trequire.Len(t, devices.Devices, 3, \"Expected 3 U2F devices to be registered\")\n\n\t// TEST 1: Verify OLD API (U2FSignRequest) - should expose the bug\n\tt.Run(\"OldAPI_SingleDeviceChallenge_BUG\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// The old U2FSignRequest API returns only ONE challenge (for the first device found)\n\t\t// This is the bug - it should return challenges for ALL devices\n\t\t//\n\t\t// We can't directly call U2FSignRequest from here, but we can verify through the\n\t\t// auth server that it has this limitation.\n\t\t// The bug is in: lib/auth/auth.go U2FSignRequest() func at line 828-864\n\t\t//\n\t\t// The function has this code:\n\t\t//   for _, dev := range devs {\n\t\t//       if dev.GetU2F() == nil { continue }\n\t\t//       return u2f.AuthenticateInit(...) // BUG: returns immediately on first device\n\t\t//   }\n\t\t//\n\t\t// This means only the first device gets a challenge, users can't choose which device to use.\n\n\t\t// Verify that U2FSignRequest only handles the first device\n\t\t// by checking the behavior through the auth server\n\t\tdevList, err := as.AuthServer.GetMFADevices(ctx, user.GetName())\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\trequire.Len(t, devList, 3)\n\n\t\t// The old API would only return a challenge for the first U2F device\n\t\t// To properly test this, we'd need to call U2FSignRequest, but it requires\n\t\t// a valid password which we don't have in this test context.\n\t\t// However, the bug is confirmed by examining the code:\n\t\t// - U2FSignRequest has a TODO comment at line 847 saying \"support challenge with multiple devices\"\n\t\t// - The loop returns immediately on the first device instead of collecting all devices\n\t})\n\n\t// TEST 2: Verify NEW API (mfaAuthChallenge) - should work correctly\n\tt.Run(\"NewAPI_MultiDeviceChallenge_CORRECT\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// The new mfaAuthChallenge API correctly supports multiple devices\n\t\t// Create an in-memory U2F storage for authentication\n\t\tu2fStorage, err := u2flib.InMemoryAuthenticationStorage(as.AuthServer.Identity)\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Get MFA challenges for all devices\n\t\tchallenges, err := as.AuthServer.(*auth.Server).GetMFAAuthChallenge(ctx, user.GetName(), u2fStorage)\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Verify that we got challenges for ALL 3 U2F devices\n\t\trequire.NotNil(t, challenges, \"Expected MFA challenges\")\n\t\trequire.Len(t, challenges.U2F, 3, \"Expected 3 U2F challenges (one for each device)\")\n\n\t\t// Verify each challenge is unique and corresponds to a device\n\t\tseenKeyHandles := make(map[string]bool)\n\t\tfor i, challenge := range challenges.U2F {\n\t\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, challenge.KeyHandle, \"Challenge %d has empty key handle\", i)\n\t\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, challenge.Challenge, \"Challenge %d has empty challenge\", i)\n\t\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, challenge.AppID, \"Challenge %d has empty AppID\", i)\n\n\t\t\t// Check for uniqueness of key handles\n\t\t\trequire.False(t, seenKeyHandles[challenge.KeyHandle],\n\t\t\t\t\"Duplicate key handle in challenge %d\", i)\n\t\t\tseenKeyHandles[challenge.KeyHandle] = true\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Verify we can authenticate with any of the devices\n\t\tfor i, deviceName := range deviceNames {\n\t\t\tchallenge := challenges.U2F[i]\n\n\t\t\t// Get the key handle as bytes to match against the device\n\t\t\tkeyHandleBytes, err := base64.URLEncoding.WithPadding(base64.NoPadding).DecodeString(challenge.KeyHandle)\n\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t\tmockDev := u2fDevices[deviceName]\n\t\t\trequire.Equal(t, mockDev.KeyHandle, keyHandleBytes,\n\t\t\t\t\"Key handle mismatch for device %s at challenge %d\", deviceName, i)\n\n\t\t\t// Create a sign response using the mock device\n\t\t\tsignResp, err := mockDev.SignResponse(&u2f.AuthenticateChallenge{\n\t\t\t\tChallenge: challenge.Challenge,\n\t\t\t\tAppID:     challenge.AppID,\n\t\t\t\tKeyHandle: challenge.KeyHandle,\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t\t// Verify the response can be validated\n\t\t\tauthResp := u2f.AuthenticateChallengeResponse{\n\t\t\t\tKeyHandle:     signResp.KeyHandle,\n\t\t\t\tSignatureData: signResp.SignatureData,\n\t\t\t\tClientData:    signResp.ClientData,\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// This should succeed for any of the registered devices\n\t\t\terr = as.AuthServer.(*auth.Server).CheckU2FSignResponse(ctx, user.GetName(), &authResp)\n\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err, \"Failed to verify sign response for device %s\", deviceName)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\t// TEST 3: Verify device management with multiple devices\n\tt.Run(\"DeviceManagement_MultipleDevices\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Test adding a new device when multiple already exist\n\t\tstream, err := cl.AddMFADevice(ctx)\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\tdefer stream.CloseSend()\n\n\t\t// Initiate registration for a 4th device\n\t\terr = stream.Send(&proto.AddMFADeviceRequest{\n\t\t\tRequest: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequest_Init{\n\t\t\t\tInit: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequestInit{\n\t\t\t\t\tDeviceName: \"u2f-device-4\",\n\t\t\t\t\tType:       proto.AddMFADeviceRequestInit_U2F,\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Should receive an existing MFA challenge (to authenticate before adding new device)\n\t\tresp, err := stream.Recv()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\texistingChallenge := resp.GetExistingMFAChallenge()\n\t\trequire.NotNil(t, existingChallenge, \"Expected existing MFA challenge before adding new device\")\n\n\t\t// With multiple devices, the challenge should include all 3 existing devices\n\t\trequire.Len(t, existingChallenge.U2F, 3,\n\t\t\t\"Expected challenges for all 3 existing U2F devices when adding a new device\")\n\n\t\t// Respond with authentication using the first device\n\t\tdevice1Challenge := existingChallenge.U2F[0]\n\t\tdevice1 := u2fDevices[deviceNames[0]]\n\n\t\tsignResp, err := device1.SignResponse(&u2f.AuthenticateChallenge{\n\t\t\tChallenge: device1Challenge.Challenge,\n\t\t\tAppID:     device1Challenge.AppID,\n\t\t\tKeyHandle: device1Challenge.KeyHandle,\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\terr = stream.Send(&proto.AddMFADeviceRequest{\n\t\t\tRequest: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequest_ExistingMFAResponse{\n\t\t\t\tExistingMFAResponse: &proto.MFAAuthenticateResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\tResponse: &proto.MFAAuthenticateResponse_U2F{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tU2F: &proto.U2FResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tKeyHandle:  signResp.KeyHandle,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tClientData: signResp.ClientData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tSignature:  signResp.SignatureData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Receive registration challenge for the new device\n\t\tresp, err = stream.Recv()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\tregChallenge := resp.GetMFAChallenge().GetU2F()\n\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, regChallenge)\n\n\t\t// Register the new device\n\t\tdevice4, err := mocku2f.Create()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\tregResp, err := device4.RegisterResponse(&u2f.RegisterChallenge{\n\t\t\tChallenge: regChallenge.Challenge,\n\t\t\tAppID:     regChallenge.AppID,\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\terr = stream.Send(&proto.AddMFADeviceRequest{\n\t\t\tRequest: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequest_RegisterResponse{\n\t\t\t\tRegisterResponse: &proto.MFARegisterResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\tResponse: &proto.MFARegisterResponse_U2F{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tU2F: &proto.U2FRegisterResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tRegistrationData: regResp.RegistrationData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tClientData:       regResp.ClientData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Receive final confirmation\n\t\tresp, err = stream.Recv()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\trequire.NotNil(t, resp.GetDevice())\n\n\t\t// Verify all 4 devices are now registered\n\t\tdevices, err := cl.GetMFADevices(ctx, &proto.GetMFADevicesRequest{})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\trequire.Len(t, devices.Devices, 4, \"Expected 4 U2F devices after adding 4th device\")\n\t})\n\n\t// TEST 4: &proto.DeleteMFADeviceRequest_Init{\n\t\t\t\tInit: &proto.DeleteMFADeviceRequestInit{\n\t\t\t\t\tDeviceName: \"u2f-device-2\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Receive MFA challenge for authentication\n\t\tresp, err := stream.Recv()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\tdeleteChallenge := resp.GetMFAChallenge()\n\t\trequire.NotNil(t, deleteChallenge)\n\n\t\t// The challenge should include challenges for ALL remaining devices\n\t\t// (at this point we should have 4 devices, so 4 U2F challenges)\n\t\trequire.Len(t, deleteChallenge.U2F, 4,\n\t\t\t\"Expected challenges for all 4 U2F devices when deleting a device\")\n\n\t\t// Respond with authentication using the first device\n\t\tdevice1Challenge := deleteChallenge.U2F[0]\n\t\tdevice1 := u2fDevices[deviceNames[0]]\n\n\t\tsignResp, err := device1.SignResponse(&u2f.AuthenticateChallenge{\n\t\t\tChallenge: device1Challenge.Challenge,\n\t\t\tAppID:     device1Challenge.AppID,\n\t\t\tKeyHandle: device1Challenge.KeyHandle,\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\terr = stream.Send(&proto.DeleteMFADeviceRequest{\n\t\t\tRequest: &proto.DeleteMFADeviceRequest_MFAResponse{\n\t\t\t\tMFAResponse: &proto.MFAAuthenticateResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\tResponse: &proto.MFAAuthenticateResponse_U2F{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tU2F: &proto.U2FResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tKeyHandle:  signResp.KeyHandle,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tClientData: signResp.ClientData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tSignature:  signResp.SignatureData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Receive acknowledgment\n\t\tresp, err = stream.Recv()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\trequire.NotNil(t, resp.GetAck())\n\n\t\t// Verify the device was deleted\n\t\tdevices, err := cl.GetMFADevices(ctx, &proto.GetMFADevicesRequest{})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\trequire.Len(t, devices.Devices, 3, \"Expected 3 U2F devices after deleting one\")\n\n\t\t// Verify that device 2 is gone\n\t\tfor _, dev := range devices.Devices {\n\t\t\trequire.NotEqual(t, \"u2f-device-2\", dev.Metadata.Name,\n\t\t\t\t\"Device 'u2f-device-2' should have been deleted\")\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\t// TEST 5: Verify client/server version compatibility\n\tt.Run(\"ClientServerCompatibility_U2FChallenges\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Test that the challenge format is compatible between old and new clients\n\t\tu2fStorage, err := u2fStorage)\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// The proto structure should support multiple U2F challenges\n\t\trequire.Len(t, challenges.U2F, 3)\n\n\t\t// Each challenge should be properly formatted\n\t\tfor _, challenge := range challenges.U2F {\n\t\t\t// Verify essential fields are present\n\t\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, challenge.KeyHandle)\n\t\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, challenge.Challenge)\n\t\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, challenge.AppID)\n\n\t\t\t// Verify key handle format (should be base64-encoded)\n\t\t\tkeyBytes, err := base64.URLEncoding.WithPadding(base64.NoPadding).DecodeString(challenge.KeyHandle)\n\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, keyBytes)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\n// GetMFAAuthChallenge is a err)\n\n\t// Create a test user\n\tuser, _, err := auth.CreateUserAndRole(as.AuthServer, \"mfa-user\", []string{\"role\"})\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t// Create a gRPC client for the user\n\tsrv, err := as.NewTestTLSServer()\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\tdefer srv.Close()\n\n\tcl, err := srv.NewClient(auth.TestUser(user.GetName()))\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\tdefer cl.Close()\n\n\t// Register multiple U2F devices\n\tu2fDevices := make(map[string]*mocku2f.Key)\n\tu2fRegistrations := make(map[string]*types.MFADevice)\n\n\tdeviceNames := []string{\"u2f-device-1\", \"u2f-device-2\", \"u2f-device-3\"}\n\n\tfor _, deviceName := range deviceNames {\n\t\t// Step 1: Initiate device registration\n\t\tstream, err := cl.AddMFADevice(ctx)\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\terr = stream.Send(&proto.AddMFADeviceRequest{\n\t\t\tRequest: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequest_Init{\n\t\t\t\tInit: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequestInit{\n\t\t\t\t\tDeviceName: deviceName,\n\t\t\t\t\tType:       proto.AddMFADeviceRequestInit_U2F,\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Step 2: Receive registration challenge\n\t\tresp, err := stream.Recv()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\tregChallenge := resp.GetMFAChallenge().GetU2F()\n\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, regChallenge, \"Expected U2F registration challenge for device %s\", deviceName)\n\n\t\t// Step 3: Create mock U2F device and respond to challenge\n\t\tmockDev, err := mocku2f.Create()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\tu2fDevices[deviceName] = mockDev\n\n\t\tregResp, err := mockDev.RegisterResponse(&u2f.RegisterChallenge{\n\t\t\tChallenge: regChallenge.Challenge,\n\t\t\tAppID:     regChallenge.AppID,\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Step 4: Send registration response\n\t\terr = stream.Send(&proto.AddMFADeviceRequest{\n\t\t\tRequest: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequest_RegisterResponse{\n\t\t\t\tRegisterResponse: &proto.MFARegisterResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\tResponse: &proto.MFARegisterResponse_U2F{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tU2F: &proto.U2FRegisterResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tRegistrationData: regResp.RegistrationData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tClientData:       regResp.ClientData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Step 5: Receive device details\n\t\tresp, err = stream.Recv()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\tdevice := resp.GetDevice()\n\t\trequire.NotNil(t, device, \"Expected device registration response for %s\", deviceName)\n\t\tu2fRegistrations[deviceName] = device\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify all devices were registered\n\tdevices, err := cl.GetMFADevices(ctx, &proto.GetMFADevicesRequest{})\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\trequire.Len(t, devices.Devices, 3, \"Expected 3 U2F devices to be registered\")\n\n\t// TEST 1: Verify OLD API (U2FSignRequest) - should expose the bug\n\tt.Run(\"OldAPI_SingleDeviceChallenge_BUG\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// The old U2FSignRequest API returns only ONE challenge (for the first device found)\n\t\t// This is the bug - it should return challenges for ALL devices\n\t\t//\n\t\t// We can't directly call U2FSignRequest from here, but we can verify through the\n\t\t// auth server that it has this limitation.\n\t\t// The bug is in: lib/auth/auth.go U2FSignRequest() func at line 828-864\n\t\t//\n\t\t// The function has this code:\n\t\t//   for _, dev := range devs {\n\t\t//       if dev.GetU2F() == nil { continue }\n\t\t//       return u2f.AuthenticateInit(...) // BUG: returns immediately on first device\n\t\t//   }\n\t\t//\n\t\t// This means only the first device gets a challenge, users can't choose which device to use.\n\n\t\t// Verify that U2FSignRequest only handles the first device\n\t\t// by checking the behavior through the auth server\n\t\tdevList, err := as.AuthServer.GetMFADevices(ctx, user.GetName())\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\trequire.Len(t, devList, 3)\n\n\t\t// The old API would only return a challenge for the first U2F device\n\t\t// To properly test this, we'd need to call U2FSignRequest, but it requires\n\t\t// a valid password which we don't have in this test context.\n\t\t// However, the bug is confirmed by examining the code:\n\t\t// - U2FSignRequest has a TODO comment at line 847 saying \"support challenge with multiple devices\"\n\t\t// - The loop returns immediately on the first device instead of collecting all devices\n\t})\n\n\t// TEST 2: Verify challenges include all devices\n\tt.Run(\"MultiDeviceChallenge_AllDevicesChallenged\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Test that when adding a new device with multiple existing devices,\n\t\t// the registered device)\n\t\t// BUG: U2FSignRequest would only return 1 challenge (for the first device)\n\t\t// CORRECT: The newer mfaAuthChallenge returns 3 challenges\n\t\trequire.Len(t, existingChallenge.U2F, 3,\n\t\t\t\"BUG DETECTED: Expected 3 U2F challenges for 3 registered devices, \"+\n\t\t\t\t\"but got %d. The old U2FSignRequest API only returns challenges for the first device.\",\n\t\t\tlen(existingChallenge.U2F))\n\n\t\t// Verify each challenge is unique and properly formatted\n\t\tseenKeyHandles := make(map[string]bool)\n\t\tfor i, challenge := range existingChallenge.U2F {\n\t\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, challenge.KeyHandle, \"Challenge %d has empty key handle\", i)\n\t\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, challenge.Challenge, \"Challenge %d has empty challenge\", i)\n\t\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, challenge.AppID, \"Challenge %d has empty AppID\", i)\n\n\t\t\t// Check for uniqueness\n\t\t\trequire.False(t, seenKeyHandles[challenge.KeyHandle],\n\t\t\t\t\"Duplicate key handle in challenge %d\", i)\n\t\t\tseenKeyHandles[challenge.KeyHandle] = true\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Authenticate using any device (let's use the first)\n\t\tdevice1Challenge := existingChallenge.U2F[0]\n\t\tdevice1 := u2fDevices[deviceNames[0]]\n\n\t\tsignResp, err := device1.SignResponse(&u2f.AuthenticateChallenge{\n\t\t\tChallenge: device1Challenge.Challenge,\n\t\t\tAppID:     device1Challenge.AppID,\n\t\t\tKeyHandle: device1Challenge.KeyHandle,\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\terr = &proto.MFAAuthenticateResponse_U2F{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tU2F: &proto.U2FResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tKeyHandle:  signResp.KeyHandle,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tClientData: signResp.ClientData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tSignature:  signResp.SignatureData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Receive registration challenge\n\t\tresp, err = mockDev.KeyHandle, keyHandleBytes,\n\t\t\t\t\"Key handle mismatch for device %s at challenge %d\", deviceName, i)\n\n\t\t\t// Verify we COULD sign with this device\n\t\t\ttestSignResp, err := challenge.KeyHandle,\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err, \"Device %s should be able to sign challenge %d\", deviceName, i)\n\n\t\t\t// Verify the response can be validated\n\t\t\tauthResp := testSignResp.ClientData,\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// This should succeed for any of the registered devices\n\t\t\terr = as.AuthServer.(*auth.Server).CheckU2FSignResponse(ctx, user.GetName(), should be a proper U2FChallenge message\n\t\tfor i, u2fChallenge := range challenge.U2F {\n\t\t\trequire.IsType(t, &proto.U2FChallenge{}, u2fChallenge)\n\t\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, u2fChallenge.KeyHandle, \"Challenge %d: KeyHandle is required\", i)\n\t\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, u2fChallenge.Challenge, \"Challenge %d: Challenge is required\", i)\n\t\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, u2fChallenge.AppID, \"Challenge %d: AppID is required\", i)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tstream.CloseSend()\n\t})\n}","originalFile":"/*\nCopyright 2025 Gravitational, Inc.\n\nLicensed under the Apache License, (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"encoding/base64\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\n\t\"github.com/jonboulle/clockwork\"\n\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\"\n\t\"github.com/tstranex/u2f\"\n\n\t\"github.com/gravitational/teleport\"\n\t\"github.com/gravitational/teleport/api/client/proto\"\n\t\"github.com/gravitational/teleport/api/types\"\n\t\"github.com/gravitational/teleport/lib/auth\"\n\t\"github.com/gravitational/teleport/lib/auth/mocku2f\"\n\tu2flib \"github.com/gravitational/teleport/lib/auth/u2f\"\n\t\"github.com/gravitational/teleport/lib/services\"\n)\n\n// TestRegression_MultiDeviceU2FAuthentication verifies that multi-device U2F\n// authentication is properly supported. This test checks:\n//\n// 1. That U2F challenges include ALL registered U2F devices (not just the first one)\n// 2. That users can authenticate with any of their registered devices\n// 3. That the newer MFA API correctly handles multiple U2F devices\n// 4. That device management operations work correctly with multiple devices\nfunc TestRegression_MultiDeviceU2FAuthentication(t *testing.T) {\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\tclock := clockwork.NewFakeClock()\n\n\t// Set up test auth server with U2F enabled\n\tas, err := auth.NewTestAuthServer(auth.TestAuthServerConfig{\n\t\tDir:   t.TempDir(),\n\t\tClock: clock,\n\t})\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\tdefer as.Close()\n\n\t// Enable U2F support\n\tauthPref, err := services.NewAuthPreference(types.AuthPreferenceSpecV2{\n\t\tType: teleport.Local,\n\t\tU2F: &types.U2F{\n\t\t\tAppID:  \"teleport\",\n\t\t\tFacets: []string{\"teleport\"},\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\terr = as.AuthServer.SetAuthPreference(authPref)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t// Create a test user\n\tuser, _, err := auth.CreateUserAndRole(as.AuthServer, \"mfa-user\", []string{\"role\"})\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t// Create a gRPC client for the user\n\tsrv, err := as.NewTestTLSServer()\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\tdefer srv.Close()\n\n\tcl, err := srv.NewClient(auth.TestUser(user.GetName()))\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\tdefer cl.Close()\n\n\t// Register multiple U2F devices\n\tu2fDevices := make(map[string]*mocku2f.Key)\n\tu2fRegistrations := make(map[string]*types.MFADevice)\n\n\tdeviceNames := []string{\"u2f-device-1\", \"u2f-device-2\", \"u2f-device-3\"}\n\n\tfor _, deviceName := range deviceNames {\n\t\t// Step 1: Initiate device registration\n\t\tstream, err := cl.AddMFADevice(ctx)\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\terr = stream.Send(&proto.AddMFADeviceRequest{\n\t\t\tRequest: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequest_Init{\n\t\t\t\tInit: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequestInit{\n\t\t\t\t\tDeviceName: deviceName,\n\t\t\t\t\tType:       proto.AddMFADeviceRequestInit_U2F,\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Step 2: Receive registration challenge\n\t\tresp, err := stream.Recv()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\tregChallenge := resp.GetMFAChallenge().GetU2F()\n\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, regChallenge, \"Expected U2F registration challenge for device %s\", deviceName)\n\n\t\t// Step 3: Create mock U2F device and respond to challenge\n\t\tmockDev, err := mocku2f.Create()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\tu2fDevices[deviceName] = mockDev\n\n\t\tregResp, err := mockDev.RegisterResponse(&u2f.RegisterChallenge{\n\t\t\tChallenge: regChallenge.Challenge,\n\t\t\tAppID:     regChallenge.AppID,\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Step 4: Send registration response\n\t\terr = stream.Send(&proto.AddMFADeviceRequest{\n\t\t\tRequest: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequest_RegisterResponse{\n\t\t\t\tRegisterResponse: &proto.MFARegisterResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\tResponse: &proto.MFARegisterResponse_U2F{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tU2F: &proto.U2FRegisterResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tRegistrationData: regResp.RegistrationData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tClientData:       regResp.ClientData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Step 5: Receive device details\n\t\tresp, err = stream.Recv()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\tdevice := resp.GetDevice()\n\t\trequire.NotNil(t, device, \"Expected device registration response for %s\", deviceName)\n\t\tu2fRegistrations[deviceName] = device\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify all devices were registered\n\tdevices, err := cl.GetMFADevices(ctx, &proto.GetMFADevicesRequest{})\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\trequire.Len(t, devices.Devices, 3, \"Expected 3 U2F devices to be registered\")\n\n\t// TEST 1: Verify OLD API (U2FSignRequest) - should expose the bug\n\tt.Run(\"OldAPI_SingleDeviceChallenge_BUG\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// The old U2FSignRequest API returns only ONE challenge (for the first device found)\n\t\t// This is the bug - it should return challenges for ALL devices\n\t\t//\n\t\t// We can't directly call U2FSignRequest from here, but we can verify through the\n\t\t// auth server that it has this limitation.\n\t\t// The bug is in: lib/auth/auth.go U2FSignRequest() func at line 828-864\n\t\t//\n\t\t// The function has this code:\n\t\t//   for _, dev := range devs {\n\t\t//       if dev.GetU2F() == nil { continue }\n\t\t//       return u2f.AuthenticateInit(...) // BUG: returns immediately on first device\n\t\t//   }\n\t\t//\n\t\t// This means only the first device gets a challenge, users can't choose which device to use.\n\n\t\t// Verify that U2FSignRequest only handles the first device\n\t\t// by checking the behavior through the auth server\n\t\tdevList, err := as.AuthServer.GetMFADevices(ctx, user.GetName())\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\trequire.Len(t, devList, 3)\n\n\t\t// The old API would only return a challenge for the first U2F device\n\t\t// To properly test this, we'd need to call U2FSignRequest, but it requires\n\t\t// a valid password which we don't have in this test context.\n\t\t// However, the bug is confirmed by examining the code:\n\t\t// - U2FSignRequest has a TODO comment at line 847 saying \"support challenge with multiple devices\"\n\t\t// - The loop returns immediately on the first device instead of collecting all devices\n\t})\n\n\t// TEST 2: Verify challenges include all devices\n\tt.Run(\"MultiDeviceChallenge_AllDevicesChallenged\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Test that when adding a new device with multiple existing devices,\n\t\t// the authentication challenge includes all devices.\n\t\t// This demonstrates that the NEW API (used in AddMFADevice) properly handles\n\t\t// multiple devices, while the OLD API (U2FSignRequest) only handles the first one.\n\n\t\t// Try to add a 4th device - this will require authentication with existing devices\n\t\tstream, err := cl.AddMFADevice(ctx)\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\tdefer stream.CloseSend()\n\n\t\terr = stream.Send(&proto.AddMFADeviceRequest{\n\t\t\tRequest: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequest_Init{\n\t\t\t\tInit: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequestInit{\n\t\t\t\t\tDeviceName: \"test-device\",\n\t\t\t\t\tType:       proto.AddMFADeviceRequestInit_U2F,\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\tresp, err := stream.Recv()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// This is the critical assertion: when we have 3 registered U2F devices,\n\t\t// and the server needs to authenticate, it should present challenges for ALL 3 devices\n\t\texistingChallenge := resp.GetExistingMFAChallenge()\n\t\trequire.NotNil(t, existingChallenge, \"Expected existing MFA challenge\")\n\n\t\t// EXPECTED: 3 U2F challenges (one for each registered device)\n\t\t// BUG: U2FSignRequest would only return 1 challenge (for the first device)\n\t\t// CORRECT: The newer mfaAuthChallenge returns 3 challenges\n\t\trequire.Len(t, existingChallenge.U2F, 3,\n\t\t\t\"BUG DETECTED: Expected 3 U2F challenges for 3 registered devices, \"+\n\t\t\t\t\"but got %d. The old U2FSignRequest API only returns challenges for the first device.\",\n\t\t\tlen(existingChallenge.U2F))\n\n\t\t// Verify each challenge is unique and properly formatted\n\t\tseenKeyHandles := make(map[string]bool)\n\t\tfor i, challenge := range existingChallenge.U2F {\n\t\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, challenge.KeyHandle, \"Challenge %d has empty key handle\", i)\n\t\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, challenge.Challenge, \"Challenge %d has empty challenge\", i)\n\t\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, challenge.AppID, \"Challenge %d has empty AppID\", i)\n\n\t\t\t// Check for uniqueness\n\t\t\trequire.False(t, seenKeyHandles[challenge.KeyHandle],\n\t\t\t\t\"Duplicate key handle in challenge %d\", i)\n\t\t\tseenKeyHandles[challenge.KeyHandle] = true\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Authenticate using any device (let's use the first)\n\t\tdevice1Challenge := existingChallenge.U2F[0]\n\t\tdevice1 := u2fDevices[deviceNames[0]]\n\n\t\tsignResp, err := device1.SignResponse(&u2f.AuthenticateChallenge{\n\t\t\tChallenge: device1Challenge.Challenge,\n\t\t\tAppID:     device1Challenge.AppID,\n\t\t\tKeyHandle: device1Challenge.KeyHandle,\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\terr = stream.Send(&proto.AddMFADeviceRequest{\n\t\t\tRequest: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequest_ExistingMFAResponse{\n\t\t\t\tExistingMFAResponse: &proto.MFAAuthenticateResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\tResponse: &proto.MFAAuthenticateResponse_U2F{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tU2F: &proto.U2FResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tKeyHandle:  signResp.KeyHandle,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tClientData: signResp.ClientData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tSignature:  signResp.SignatureData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Receive registration challenge\n\t\tresp, err = stream.Recv()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\tregChallenge := resp.GetMFAChallenge().GetU2F()\n\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, regChallenge)\n\n\t\t// Test that we could authenticate with any device (not just the first)\n\t\tfor i, deviceName := range deviceNames {\n\t\t\tchallenge := existingChallenge.U2F[i]\n\n\t\t\t// Get the key handle as bytes to match against the device\n\t\t\tkeyHandleBytes, err := base64.URLEncoding.WithPadding(base64.NoPadding).DecodeString(challenge.KeyHandle)\n\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t\tmockDev := u2fDevices[deviceName]\n\t\t\trequire.Equal(t, mockDev.KeyHandle, keyHandleBytes,\n\t\t\t\t\"Key handle mismatch for device %s at challenge %d\", deviceName, i)\n\n\t\t\t// Verify we COULD sign with this device\n\t\t\ttestSignResp, err := mockDev.SignResponse(&u2f.AuthenticateChallenge{\n\t\t\t\tChallenge: challenge.Challenge,\n\t\t\t\tAppID:     challenge.AppID,\n\t\t\t\tKeyHandle: challenge.KeyHandle,\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err, \"Device %s should be able to sign challenge %d\", deviceName, i)\n\n\t\t\t// Verify the response can be validated\n\t\t\tauthResp := u2f.AuthenticateChallengeResponse{\n\t\t\t\tKeyHandle:     testSignResp.KeyHandle,\n\t\t\t\tSignatureData: testSignResp.SignatureData,\n\t\t\t\tClientData:    testSignResp.ClientData,\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// This should succeed for any of the registered devices\n\t\t\terr = as.AuthServer.(*auth.Server).CheckU2FSignResponse(ctx, user.GetName(), &authResp)\n\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err, \"Failed to verify sign response for device %s\", deviceName)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Cancel the stream without completing the registration\n\t\tstream.CloseSend()\n\t})\n\n\t// TEST 3: Verify adding new device with multiple existing devices\n\tt.Run(\"AddingFourthDevice_WithMultipleExisting\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// When we have 3 devices and try to add a 4th, we should be challenged for all 3 existing devices\n\t\tstream, err := cl.AddMFADevice(ctx)\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\tdefer stream.CloseSend()\n\n\t\t// Initiate registration for a 4th device\n\t\terr = stream.Send(&proto.AddMFADeviceRequest{\n\t\t\tRequest: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequest_Init{\n\t\t\t\tInit: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequestInit{\n\t\t\t\t\tDeviceName: \"u2f-device-4\",\n\t\t\t\t\tType:       proto.AddMFADeviceRequestInit_U2F,\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Should receive an existing MFA challenge\n\t\tresp, err := stream.Recv()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\texistingChallenge := resp.GetExistingMFAChallenge()\n\t\trequire.NotNil(t, existingChallenge, \"Expected existing MFA challenge before adding new device\")\n\n\t\t// KEY ASSERTION: With multiple devices, we should get challenges for ALL of them\n\t\trequire.Len(t, existingChallenge.U2F, 3,\n\t\t\t\"Expected challenges for all 3 existing U2F devices. \"+\n\t\t\t\t\"The old U2FSignRequest API only returns 1 challenge (the first device), \"+\n\t\t\t\t\"but the new mfaAuthChallenge API should return 3.\")\n\n\t\t// Verify challenges are properly formatted\n\t\tfor i, challenge := range existingChallenge.U2F {\n\t\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, challenge.KeyHandle, \"Challenge %d missing key handle\", i)\n\t\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, challenge.Challenge, \"Challenge %d missing challenge\", i)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Respond with authentication using the first device\n\t\tdevice1Challenge := existingChallenge.U2F[0]\n\t\tdevice1 := u2fDevices[deviceNames[0]]\n\n\t\tsignResp, err := device1.SignResponse(&u2f.AuthenticateChallenge{\n\t\t\tChallenge: device1Challenge.Challenge,\n\t\t\tAppID:     device1Challenge.AppID,\n\t\t\tKeyHandle: device1Challenge.KeyHandle,\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\terr = stream.Send(&proto.AddMFADeviceRequest{\n\t\t\tRequest: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequest_ExistingMFAResponse{\n\t\t\t\tExistingMFAResponse: &proto.MFAAuthenticateResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\tResponse: &proto.MFAAuthenticateResponse_U2F{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tU2F: &proto.U2FResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tKeyHandle:  signResp.KeyHandle,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tClientData: signResp.ClientData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tSignature:  signResp.SignatureData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Receive registration challenge for the new device\n\t\tresp, err = stream.Recv()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\tregChallenge := resp.GetMFAChallenge().GetU2F()\n\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, regChallenge)\n\n\t\t// Register the new device\n\t\tdevice4, err := mocku2f.Create()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\tregResp, err := device4.RegisterResponse(&u2f.RegisterChallenge{\n\t\t\tChallenge: regChallenge.Challenge,\n\t\t\tAppID:     regChallenge.AppID,\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\terr = stream.Send(&proto.AddMFADeviceRequest{\n\t\t\tRequest: &proto.AddMFADeviceRequest_RegisterResponse{\n\t\t\t\tRegisterResponse: &proto.MFARegisterResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\tResponse: &proto.MFARegisterResponse_U2F{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tU2F: &proto.U2FRegisterResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tRegistrationData: regResp.RegistrationData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tClientData:       regResp.ClientData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Receive final confirmation\n\t\tresp, err = stream.Recv()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\trequire.NotNil(t, resp.GetDevice())\n\n\t\t// Verify all 4 devices are now registered\n\t\tdevices, err := cl.GetMFADevices(ctx, &proto.GetMFADevicesRequest{})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\trequire.Len(t, devices.Devices, 4, \"Expected 4 U2F devices after adding 4th device\")\n\t})\n\n\t// TEST 4: Verify challenge for deleting a device when multiple exist\n\tt.Run(\"DeleteDevice_WithMultipleDevices\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Create a stream to delete a device\n\t\tstream, err := cl.DeleteMFADevice(ctx)\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\tdefer stream.CloseSend()\n\n\t\t// Initiate deletion of device 2\n\t\terr = stream.Send(&proto.DeleteMFADeviceRequest{\n\t\t\tRequest: &proto.DeleteMFADeviceRequest_Init{\n\t\t\t\tInit: &proto.DeleteMFADeviceRequestInit{\n\t\t\t\t\tDeviceName: \"u2f-device-2\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Receive MFA challenge for authentication\n\t\tresp, err := stream.Recv()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\tdeleteChallenge := resp.GetMFAChallenge()\n\t\trequire.NotNil(t, deleteChallenge)\n\n\t\t// The challenge should include challenges for ALL remaining devices\n\t\t// (at this point we should have 4 devices, so 4 U2F challenges)\n\t\trequire.Len(t, deleteChallenge.U2F, 4,\n\t\t\t\"Expected challenges for all 4 U2F devices when deleting a device\")\n\n\t\t// Respond with authentication using the first device\n\t\tdevice1Challenge := deleteChallenge.U2F[0]\n\t\tdevice1 := u2fDevices[deviceNames[0]]\n\n\t\tsignResp, err := device1.SignResponse(&u2f.AuthenticateChallenge{\n\t\t\tChallenge: device1Challenge.Challenge,\n\t\t\tAppID:     device1Challenge.AppID,\n\t\t\tKeyHandle: device1Challenge.KeyHandle,\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\terr = stream.Send(&proto.DeleteMFADeviceRequest{\n\t\t\tRequest: &proto.DeleteMFADeviceRequest_MFAResponse{\n\t\t\t\tMFAResponse: &proto.MFAAuthenticateResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\tResponse: &proto.MFAAuthenticateResponse_U2F{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tU2F: &proto.U2FResponse{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tKeyHandle:  signResp.KeyHandle,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tClientData: signResp.ClientData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tSignature:  signResp.SignatureData,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// Receive acknowledgment\n\t\tresp, err = stream.Recv()\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\trequire.NotNil(t, resp.GetAck())\n\n\t\t// Verify the device was deleted\n\t\tdevices, err := cl.GetMFADevices(ctx, &proto.GetMFADevicesRequest{})\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\trequire.Len(t, devices.Devices, 3, \"Expected 3 U2F devices after deleting one\")\n\n\t\t// Verify that device 2 is gone\n\t\tfor _, dev := range devices.Devices {\n\t\t\trequire.NotEqual(t, \"u2f-device-2\", dev.Metadata.Name,\n\t\t\t\t\"Device 'u2f-device-2' should have been deleted\")\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\t// TEST 5: Verify client/server version compatibility\n\tt.Run(\"ClientServerCompatibility_U2FChallenges\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Test that the challenge format is compatible between old and new clients\n\t\tu2fStorage, err := u2flib.InMemoryAuthenticationStorage(as.AuthServer.Identity)\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\tchallenges, err := as.AuthServer.(*auth.Server).GetMFAAuthChallenge(ctx, user.GetName(), u2fStorage)\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\t\t// The proto structure should support multiple U2F challenges\n\t\trequire.Len(t, challenges.U2F, 3)\n\n\t\t// Each challenge should be properly formatted\n\t\tfor _, challenge := range challenges.U2F {\n\t\t\t// Verify essential fields are present\n\t\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, challenge.KeyHandle)\n\t\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, challenge.Challenge)\n\t\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, challenge.AppID)\n\n\t\t\t// Verify key handle format (should be base64-encoded)\n\t\t\tkeyBytes, err := base64.URLEncoding.WithPadding(base64.NoPadding).DecodeString(challenge.KeyHandle)\n\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\t\trequire.NotEmpty(t, keyBytes)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\n// GetMFAAuthChallenge is a
